---
title: "birdnetR Analysis"
author: "`r Sys.info()[['user']]`"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

Analysing PAM datasets analysed using [birdnetR](https://github.com/birdnet-team/birdnetR)
___

## Setup

Load libraries

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE,results='hide'}
library(birdnetR)
library(NocMigR2)
library(MonitoR)
library(magrittr)
library(tidyverse)
```

Customise variables and meta information:

```{r, warning=FALSE}
## define path to data files
path = "D:/BirdNET/path2recordings"
## Load AudioMoth configuration (CONFIG.txt)
am_config = TRUE
## Select input directory (recurisve)
recursive =  TRUE
## Create hyperlink to extracted events?
hyperlink = TRUE
## Create spectra for extracted events?
spectro = FALSE
## Set meta data
meta_data <- BirdNET_meta(
  ## Location details
  Location = NA,
  Lat = NA,
  Lon = NA,
  ## Device details
  Device = "AudioMoth", # c('AudioMoth, 'SM')
  Micro = NA,
  ## BirdNet params
  Min_conf = 0.7,
  Overlap = 0,
  Sensitivity = 1.25,
  Slist = "BirdNET_V2.4")

## Archive information
path2archive = "~/PAM/Records/"
db = "~/PAM/db.xlsx"
keep.false = FALSE
png = FALSE
```

Rename files to datetime

```{r, eval=T}
strip_device_id(input_dir = path)
```


## Execute BirdNET

```{r BirdNET}
## list audio files
wave_files <- list.files(path, pattern = ".WAV", recursive = T, ignore.case = T, full.names = T)

## ignore subfolder 'extracted' if present
wave_files <- wave_files[!stringr::str_detect(wave_files, "extracted")]

## read species from slist
slist <- birdnetR::read_labels(system.file("extdata", "ornitho_de.txt", package = "MonitoR"))

## Run BirdNET on wave files 
predictions <- birdNET_process_batch(
  wave_files = wave_files,
  slist = slist)
```

## Retrieve BirdNET results

```{r, eval=T}
## Structure results
data <- lapply(path, BirdNET,
               am_config = T,
               recursive =  T, 
               meta = meta_data)
## extract detections
data <- lapply(path, BirdNET_extract, hyperlink = TRUE, spectro = F)
```
